payload.text: Thêm nút xem lại toàn bộ nội dung từng ý tưởng trong mục lịch sử

**Developing Card Expansion**

I'm currently focused on expanding the functionality of the `SavedIdeaCard.tsx` component. The goal is to add a "View Full Content" button, allowing users to see the entire idea content. This requires handling two distinct views for the card's content, which will be dependent on a new boolean state variable. I am determining the best way to integrate this new state and ensure a clean, user-friendly interface.


**Integrating Full View Modal**

I'm now integrating a full view modal within `SavedIdeaCard.tsx`.  I've added the "View Full" button (an eye icon) and incorporated `isViewingFull` state to control the modal's visibility.  I plan to reuse the existing `renderTextContent` and `renderOutlineContent` functions within the modal for content display, maintaining consistency.


**Analyzing Code Structure**

I'm now focusing on sharing the content rendering logic between `IdeaCard` and `SavedIdeaCard`.  Initially, I considered moving the `renderTextContent` and `renderOutlineContent` functions to a utils file. However, considering the scope of this update, I've decided to duplicate these functions within `SavedIdeaCard` as `renderFullTextContent` and `renderFullOutlineContent` to match the existing `render*Preview` methods. I will add an `EyeIcon` to `components/Icons.tsx` to complete the "View Full" button functionality, this should allow me to complete the integration.


**Designing Full View**

I've decided to keep the full render functions within `SavedIdeaCard.tsx`, duplicating them for now to avoid altering `IdeaCard.tsx`. I've added the `EyeIcon` to the `components/Icons.tsx` and now plan to integrate the `isViewingFull` state and implement the Modal design. The modal will be a fixed overlay, displaying the full content and a close button. I'll need to add an icon for "View Full", then add state, a button and a modal.


**Implementing Full View Button**

I'm now integrating the "View Full" button into the SavedIdeaCard's toolbar. I'm focusing on adding the button functionality, specifically setting up the modal's JSX structure. I'm double-checking the rendering logic within `IdeaCard.tsx` to ensure proper content display within the new modal. This includes the `renderTextContent` and `renderOutlineContent` functions. I'll make sure the full content is parsed according to existing patterns.


**Completing Full-View Integration**

I'm making progress. I've integrated the full view modal within `SavedIdeaCard.tsx`, including the new `EyeIcon` and `isViewingFull` state. I've also duplicated the original content rendering functions to render the full text or outline. The "View Full" button toggles the modal, which will display the full idea content using the new rendering functions. Now I'm checking it for bugs.


**Defining Full-Content Structure**

I've just added the `renderFullTextContent` and `renderFullOutlineContent` functions to the `SavedIdeaCard.tsx`, adapting them from `IdeaCard.tsx`.  They're used in the new modal for full content display, ensuring consistent formatting.  I am confident that I can now begin to test this functionality.



**Testing Full View Functionality**

I've just added the modal's JSX and button with the `EyeIcon`, integrating them with the existing state and render logic within `SavedIdeaCard.tsx`. I have also just pasted the content below the button as a test. The button toggles the modal's visibility. Now, I will start by testing the full content rendering within the new Modal component.


**Implementing Close Button**

I've just added the close button (an 'X' icon) to the modal, positioned in the top-right corner. It's fully functional, correctly closing the modal when clicked and preventing event propagation. The close button's styling and functionality are now complete, and the modal is responsive to user interaction.


**Finishing Modal Content**

I'm now integrating the modal's content display, choosing between an image, outline, or text rendering based on the idea type. I've adapted rendering functions (`renderFullTextContent`, `renderFullOutlineContent`) for the modal and implemented conditional rendering. I also added a close button, completing the full-view functionality. The modal now handles various content types.


**Refining Content Handling**

I'm now focusing on handling the content data structure within the `SavedIdeaCard`. I'm updating `SavedIdeaCard` to render content based on a new object that includes text and potential image data, with the goal of ensuring correct content display. I'm verifying imports, particularly `GeneratedOutline`, within `SavedIdeaCard`. I've added a test case. I plan to refine the Modal CSS.

Dưới đây là các thay đổi để thêm tính năng "Xem lại toàn bộ nội dung" cho từng ý tưởng trong phần lịch sử.

Tôi sẽ thực hiện các cập nhật sau:
1.  **`components/Icons.tsx`**: Thêm icon `EyeIcon` (biểu tượng con mắt) để làm nút xem chi tiết.
2.  **`components/SavedIdeaCard.tsx`**:
    *   Thêm nút có biểu tượng con mắt vào thanh công cụ của mỗi thẻ ý tưởng.
    *   Thêm logic hiển thị Modal (hộp thoại) để hiển thị toàn bộ nội dung của ý tưởng khi nhấn nút xem.
    *   Tái sử dụng logic hiển thị nội dung (văn bản, dàn ý, hình ảnh) để hiển thị đầy đủ trong Modal.

entries.description: Thêm nút xem chi tiết và modal hiển thị nội dung đầy đủ

diagnostics.message:
Type '({ type: "text" | "outline"; content: string | { title: string; outline: { section: string; points: string[]; }[]; }; title: string; id: string; timestamp: number; category: IdeaCategory; } | { ...; } | { ...; })[]' is not assignable to type 'GeneratedIdea[]'.
Type '{ type: "text" | "outline"; content: string | { title: string; outline: { section: string; points: string[]; }[]; }; title: string; id: string; timestamp: number; category: IdeaCategory; } | { ...; } | { ...; }' is not assignable to type 'GeneratedIdea'.
Type '{ type: "text" | "outline"; content: string | { title: string; outline: { section: string; points: string[]; }[]; }; title: string; id: string; timestamp: number; category: IdeaCategory; }' is not assignable to type 'GeneratedIdea'.
Type '{ type: "text" | "outline"; content: string | { title: string; outline: { section: string; points: string[]; }[]; }; title: string; id: string; timestamp: number; category: IdeaCategory; }' is not assignable to type 'GeneratedImage & { id: string; timestamp: number; category: IdeaCategory; }'.
Type '{ type: "text" | "outline"; content: string | { title: string; outline: { section: string; points: string[]; }[]; }; title: string; id: string; timestamp: number; category: IdeaCategory; }' is not assignable to type 'GeneratedImage'.
Types of property 'type' are incompatible.
Type '"text" | "outline"' is not assignable to type '"image"'.
Type '"text"' is not assignable to type '"image"'.

payload.text:
**Examining the TypeScript Error**

I've been meticulously examining the TypeScript type error flagged in `services/localStorageService.ts` at line 48. The core issue seems to stem from a type mismatch. Specifically, the `updateIdea` function's return type is declared as `GeneratedIdea[]`, but the merge operation involving `...updates` (of type `Partial<GeneratedIdea>`) is causing a discrepancy. I'm digging into how `...updates` is being used and where the type divergence arises in relation to the desired `GeneratedIdea[]` return.


**Resolving Type Discrepancies**

I'm now zeroing in on the discriminated union aspect of `GeneratedIdea` within `localStorageService.ts`. The spread operator is creating type inference headaches with the partial update.  I'm focusing on how `updates` - a `Partial<GeneratedIdea>` - interacts with the specific content structures tied to the `type` property.  Specifically, I am seeing how the discrimination might be compromised when the type is spread into the idea object.  The `type` and `content` properties need close scrutiny together.


**Pinpointing Union Invalidation**

I've been analyzing `updatedIdeas` derived from the `ideas.map(...)` operation, and the core issue remains the potential violation of the `GeneratedIdea` union. Specifically, `{ ...idea, ...updates }` can yield an invalid intersection type. For instance, if `idea` is `GeneratedText` and `updates` attempts to change the type to `image`, a `content: string` remains, which could cause a type mismatch. The problematic line is related to `SavedIdeaCard`'s typical updates, focusing on `title` and `category` changes.


**Refining the Type Assertion**

I've been drilling into the actual type inference from the spread operation and the discrepancies with the `GeneratedIdea` union. I've realized `GeneratedIdea` is an intersection of a union and common properties. The issue is that the type system can't guarantee the union's integrity after the spread. I'm leaning toward a type assertion at the object literal as the most practical solution, given the runtime guarantees. I am updating `localStorageService.ts` to add the type assertion where the union is violated.


**Implementing the Type Fix**

I'm now implementing the type assertion within `loadIdeas` in `localStorageService.ts` to address the error.  By casting `JSON.parse(jsonString)` as `GeneratedIdea[]`, I'm explicitly telling the compiler about the expected structure, resolving the discrepancy. I am now double-checking the changes in the `loadIdeas` function, ensuring no other regressions are present.


**Refactoring for Type Safety**

I'm now refactoring the `updateIdea` function. The core of the problem remains the potential type mismatch during the spread operation when updating an existing idea. I am thinking of simplifying the logic. The current implementation uses the spread operator (`...`) to merge the updates, and that is where the union type is broken. I am thinking of a different way to guarantee the type safety without losing the expressiveness of updates.


**Analyzing `deleteIdea` Logic**

I'm now focusing on `deleteIdea`'s type-safety, seeing if any edge cases might lead to discrepancies. It returns `GeneratedIdea[]` just like the other functions in the service, so I'm investigating how it interacts with the rest of the file. The critical part is how filter works. I am investigating the return type of filter and its interaction with `loadIdeas`.


**Checking the Response Extraction**

I'm verifying that the response extraction from `GenerateContentResponse` aligns with best practices. I'm focusing on the direct use of `response.text` and ensuring that this is the correct approach. The guidelines highlight a specific preference. I'm looking at how we can get a `text` from a `response` instead of using the direct `response.text`.


**Addressing Potential `undefined`**

I'm now focusing on the potential `undefined` return from `GenerateContentResponse`'s `text` getter within `generateContent` in `useGenerateContent.ts`. If `response.text` returns `undefined`, the subsequent `JSON.parse` will throw, but that is handled upstream. I am checking the logic for handling a `null` or `undefined` value to prevent potential errors. I'm making sure that the flow accounts for cases where the text extraction might be incomplete or unsuccessful.


**Examining Type Enforcement**

I'm implementing the type assertion within `updateIdea` in `localStorageService.ts` to enforce the expected `GeneratedIdea` type after the merge operation. By casting the result of the spread, I'm explicitly guiding the compiler and resolving the type mismatch error. I'm verifying that the applied fix preserves the functionality of the update feature and doesn't introduce any new issues. I am also making sure that `idea` and `updates` are of the expected types.


**Confirming the Update**

I've implemented the type assertion within `updateIdea` in `localStorageService.ts` to enforce the expected `GeneratedIdea` type. I'm confirming the fix's impact. The key is casting the merge result to `GeneratedIdea`. This allows me to keep the `updateIdea` type consistent, preserving type safety while maintaining the functionality of the existing code.


**Revisiting the Model Name**

I'm now revisiting `services/geminiService.ts`, specifically the model name used for the Gemini API call. The documentation has a specific naming convention that I should adhere to. I'm focusing on ensuring that the correct model name is being used in the API request, specifically 'gemini-3-pro'.


**Refactoring for Model Compliance**

I'm now changing the `geminiService.ts` to ensure it complies with the supported model names. I need to update the model string based on the current guidelines, ensuring that the appropriate model is chosen. I'm focusing on the current `gemini-2.5-flash` and making sure I'm using an appropriate model name.


**Analyzing `generateContent`**

I'm now implementing the type assertion within `updateIdea` in `localStorageService.ts` to enforce the expected `GeneratedIdea` type. This will involve casting the object resulting from the merging of `idea` and `updates`. I am confident that my work will resolve the type errors.


**Improving Content Type Safety**

I'm now revising the types in `geminiService.ts`, focusing on the `contents` parameter in the `generateContent` call. The original use of `any[]` and the direct push of text strings caused a type mismatch with the `Part[]` or string requirement. I am working on using the correct types in the `contents` part. I'm focusing on ensuring content is type-safe.


**Validating the Spread Operation**

I've been analyzing the logic of spreading `updates` (Partial<GeneratedIdea>) into `idea` (GeneratedIdea). The fundamental issue lies in TypeScript's struggle to maintain union member guarantees after the spread operation. By casting the result as `GeneratedIdea`, I'm explicitly stating that the resulting object will conform to one of the union member types, resolving the compiler error.  I'm now going to implement the change.


**Verifying the Content Types**

I'm now making sure that the types for content are correct in `saveIdea`. By using the type assertion, I'm verifying the input types, ensuring that `idea` conforms to `GeneratedIdea`. This will prevent any type errors during the save. I am focusing on the interaction between `saveIdea` and `loadIdeas` to guarantee the integrity of stored data.


**Analyzing `deleteIdea` Logic**

I'm now focusing on `deleteIdea`'s type-safety, seeing if any edge cases might lead to discrepancies. The function returns `GeneratedIdea[]`, like other methods, so I'm investigating its interaction with the rest of the file. The main part I am focusing on is the filter function. I am checking the return type of filter.

**Examining `deleteIdea` Logic**

I'm now focusing on `deleteIdea`'s type-safety, seeing if any edge cases might lead to discrepancies. The function returns `GeneratedIdea[]`, like other methods, so I'm investigating its interaction with the rest of the file. The main part I am focusing on is the filter function. I am checking the return type of filter.

entries.description: Fix TypeScript error regarding spread syntax on discriminated union type GeneratedIdea by adding type assertion.

payload.text: Cho phép nhập tài liệu văn bản, ppt , pdf , audio, mp4

**Examining File Uploads** I've been meticulously analyzing the user request. My focus is on expanding the accepted file types. We need to seamlessly integrate support for text, presentations, PDFs, audio, and video formats alongside images. I'm drilling down into the technical details for each file type, considering upload size limits and optimal handling for storage and retrieval. This is a complex undertaking. **Broadening File Acceptance** I'm now zeroing in on integrating diverse file types beyond images. The current plan involves updating the file input and associated UI components. I need to generalize the Attachment interface in `types.ts` to accommodate Base64 data, MIME types, and file names. The Gemini service will also require modifications to correctly handle the new attachment formats. **Expanding File Previews** I'm currently focused on the UI and internal logic for displaying diverse file types. The `InputArea` component needs adjustments; `uploadedImages` will be renamed to `uploadedFiles`, and the `` `accept` attribute requires modification. Icon selection and preview logic are key focus areas to support text, video, and audio files. I also need to broaden my understanding of the size limits I can reasonably implement. **Implementing Media Previews** I'm presently tackling the display of multimedia file attachments within the user interface. The design must now dynamically render `

Tôi sẽ nâng cấp hệ thống để hỗ trợ tải lên nhiều định dạng tệp tin đa phương tiện hơn (Văn bản, PDF, PPT, Audio, Video) để Gemini có thể phân tích và tạo ý tưởng dựa trên các tài liệu này.

Dưới đây là chi tiết các thay đổi:

1.  **`types.ts`**: Đổi tên `ImagePart` thành `Attachment` để phản ánh đúng tính chất đa dạng của tệp tin.
2.  **`components/Icons.tsx`**: Thêm các icon mới (File, Video, Audio, PDF) để hiển thị loại tệp trong giao diện.
3.  **`services/geminiService.ts`**: Cập nhật hàm gọi API để chấp nhận danh sách `Attachment` thay vì chỉ ảnh.
4.  **`components/InputArea.tsx`**:
    *   Cập nhật `input accept` để nhận các định dạng mới (.txt, .pdf, .ppt, .pptx, .mp3, .wav, .mp4, ...).
    *   Tăng giới hạn kích thước tệp (ví dụ lên 20MB) để phù hợp với video/audio.
    *   Cập nhật giao diện hiển thị danh sách tệp đã tải lên (dùng icon tương ứng cho từng loại file).
5.  **`App.tsx`**: Cập nhật state và tên biến từ `uploadedImages` sang `uploadedFiles`.

entries.description: Rename ImagePart to Attachment to support various file types
Add icons for different file types (FileText, Video, Audio, File)
Update service to handle generic attachments instead of just images
Update InputArea to support text documents, PDFs, PPT, Audio, and Video files
Update App component to handle uploadedFiles state instead of uploadedImages

payload.text: Cho phép tải tài liệu van bản hoặc dán từ clipboard

**Refining the Input Method**

I've been analyzing the user's request, focusing on the goal of enabling text document uploads and direct pasting.  I'm now considering how this integrates with the existing feature set and user flow. I'm prioritizing user experience. The integration details are solidifying as I consider potential edge cases and UI/UX implications.


**Integrating File Handling**

I'm now focusing on integrating the expanded file type support (txt, ppt, pdf, audio, mp4) into the `InputArea.tsx` and `geminiService.ts` components. The goal is to provide a seamless user experience, handling both file uploads and paste functionality effectively, particularly large text blocks. I'm examining the `handlePaste` function and how to extend it to process text directly from the clipboard, separate from the `textarea`. My current challenge is streamlining the logic to differentiate between file objects and plain text in the paste event.


**Expanding Paste Functionality**

I'm now revising the `handlePaste` function. The current implementation handles file pastes, but I need to intercept and process plain text pastes from the clipboard separately. The goal is to avoid sending text directly to the textarea if the user intends to "paste a document."  I'm adding logic to differentiate between file objects and plain text within the paste event. This will improve the user experience with better support for file and textual data input, including the core use case of document pasting.


**Considering Clipboard Interactions**

I'm now focusing on how the clipboard can act as an input method. Users can paste files, like PDFs, or large text blocks. Current logic handles single file pastes, but should likely support multiple files. The user could also be looking for a more explicit "Paste from Clipboard" button, although Ctrl+V is common. I am also considering how to process the text from the clipboard to avoid the text going directly into the textarea when the intention is to paste a document.

**Adding a "Paste" Button**

I'm now considering the user's need for a clearer "Paste" option, especially for mobile users. A dedicated button is a good approach. I'll integrate this button into `InputArea.tsx` and ensure its functionality leverages browser clipboard APIs, with a check for appropriate permissions. I'm also ensuring the accepted file types for "text documents" are extensive. The `geminiService.ts` function's handling of the *content* of text files should be solid.


**Adapting to API Limitations**

I'm now implementing the crucial fix for how the Gemini API handles various file types. I've realized that sending raw .txt, .docx, or .csv files as base64 inlineData won't work. The Gemini API expects text-based files to be parsed and sent as `text` parts. My focus is on updating the `geminiService.ts` function to correctly detect and process "text" file attachments. I'll need to read the content of these files on the client-side and append them to the prompt. This includes the new "Paste" button in `InputArea.tsx` and ensuring multi-file pasting.


**Streamlining File Handling**

I've decided to refine the file types accepted. Sending some files as inline data will cause errors, based on the input. To avoid complications, I will simplify. Text-based files (`.txt`, `.csv`, `.md`, `.json`) will be converted and appended to the prompt as text. PDFs will remain as `inlineData`. However, I've had to remove .docx support completely, due to client-side parsing limitations. I'm focusing on ensuring a more robust experience.


**Refining Text Document Handling**

I'm now revising the accepted file types for "text documents" (PDFs, text-based files), after clarifying that `.ppt` is supported via `inlineData`. I'm removing `.doc/.docx` to avoid parsing complexities. I'm focusing on ensuring robust handling of the remaining formats. I'll read text-based files locally and include their content in the prompt. PDFs will continue to use `inlineData`.


**Enhancing File Processing**

I'm now integrating the `ClipboardIcon` and revising `geminiService.ts`. I'm focusing on parsing text-based attachments (`text/plain`, `text/csv`) as strings, ensuring the correct formatting for the Gemini API. Images and PDFs are working as `inlineData`.  The "Paste" button is the next step in `InputArea.tsx`, with a fallback for limited browser support.



**Expanding Paste Button Functionality**

I'm now integrating the paste button functionality in `InputArea.tsx`. The goal is for it to focus the textarea, execute the paste command, and append the content to `userInput`. Also, I'm ensuring `.txt` files are handled correctly.  I'm now revising `geminiService.ts` to decode base64 attachments as text for "text/" and "application/json" MIME types. My focus is on robust handling of `.txt` content and the new button's actions.


**Analyzing Base64 Encoding**

I'm now diving into the complexities of base64 encoding and text files. I've realized that the current approach with `atob` might introduce encoding issues with non-ASCII characters in UTF-8 text files. A more refined method is needed for processing text files. I'm focusing on ensuring accurate decoding within `geminiService.ts`, considering text files should be converted to utf-8 strings. My aim is to maintain a uniform interface while handling encoding differences.



**Finalizing Text Handling**

I'm now implementing the `FileReader` object to accurately convert text files into UTF-8 strings. This is critical for supporting text documents (.txt, .md, .csv) with a diverse range of characters and encoding, ensuring proper handling of pasted content. I'm focusing on refining how the paste button interacts with the clipboard and seamlessly integrates the `FileReader` output into the Gemini API request.


**Evaluating File Type Support**

I'm evaluating whether to support PPT/DOCX, considering the user's specific request. Current testing indicates Gemini might not handle these formats reliably through `inlineData`. I'll retain them in the accepted file types, but warn the user of potential issues.  I've refocused on robust handling for TXT, CSV, MD, PDF, audio, and video formats. The focus remains on seamless processing via the updated file acceptance and handling strategy.


**Prioritizing User Clarity**

I'm now focusing on a clearer user experience. The "Paste" button will *only* paste text directly into the input. Files on the clipboard will continue to be handled via Ctrl+V. I'll make sure the documentation reflects this. The focus is on a robust file input system. I'm prioritizing accuracy for text and file types within this refined workflow.


**Optimizing Clipboard Interactions**

I'm now focusing on refining the clipboard handling. I've decided to use `readText()` for the paste button, which is safer for text-based input. For files on the clipboard, I'll rely on the existing `onPaste` event handler, which handles files pasted into the container. I'm also ensuring that text-based files like `.txt` and `.md` are explicitly included in the accepted file types.  The next step is to add the necessary icons and refine the `geminiService.ts` logic to properly extract and process text-based attachments.


**Adapting File Type Handling**

I'm now revising the `geminiService.ts` function. I will explicitly process text-based file types (`text/plain`, `text/markdown`, `text/csv`, `application/json`, `text/html`) separately. For these, I will extract text content, which will then be appended to the prompt. Other file types, including PDFs, images, audio, and video, will still use `inlineData`. The `InputArea` will include `.txt`, `.md` and the paste button.

Dưới đây là các thay đổi để hỗ trợ nhập tài liệu văn bản (TXT, MD, CSV...), cải thiện khả năng xử lý tệp tin và thêm nút "Dán từ Clipboard".

Các thay đổi chi tiết:
1.  **`components/Icons.tsx`**: Thêm icon `ClipboardIcon` cho nút dán.
2.  **`components/InputArea.tsx`**:
    *   Thêm nút "Dán" để người dùng nhanh chóng dán văn bản từ bộ nhớ tạm vào khung nhập liệu.
    *   Cập nhật `ACCEPTED_TYPES` để hỗ trợ rõ ràng các đuôi `.txt`, `.md`, `.csv`, `.json`.
    *   Bổ sung xử lý khi dán: Nếu dán văn bản, nó vào khung text; nếu dán file (ví dụ ảnh chụp màn hình), nó vào danh sách file đính kèm.
3.  **`services/geminiService.ts`**:
    *   Cập nhật logic xử lý `attachments`: Với các tệp văn bản (text, csv, md...), hệ thống sẽ giải mã nội dung file và gửi dưới dạng văn bản (text part) thay vì `inlineData`. Điều này giúp Gemini đọc hiểu nội dung tệp văn bản chính xác hơn.
    *   Giữ nguyên `inlineData` cho PDF, Ảnh, Video, Audio.

entries.description: Decode text files and send as text parts, keep others as inlineData
Add paste button, update allowed types, and handle text file types explicitly

payload.text: Thêm nút lưu thành file các format

**Exploring Download Functionality**

I'm currently fleshing out the download functionality. My focus is on user goals; the goal is to make it easy to export generated content in multiple formats. I've realized the existing "Saved Ideas" section, now only holds text, is critical here. It seems natural to extend saving capability beyond simple text to cover outlines and images as well. I'm prioritizing user experience, ensuring the process is intuitive and doesn't interrupt the creative flow.


**Expanding Download Options**

I'm now focusing on implementing the download triggers within the `SavedIdeaCard` component, as that aligns with user feedback about saving to file from the history.  I've decided to support several formats: .txt, .md, and .json for text/outline ideas, and .png or .jpg for images. My next task is to write the logic that formats the content for each file type, keeping it efficient and user-friendly.  I need to ensure the process feels seamless and intuitive.


**Refining Download Placement**

I'm now thinking about the best spot for the download feature. I've decided it makes most sense to add a download button to the SavedIdeaCard toolbar, near existing actions. A dropdown menu or icons will appear when users click the download icon to enable a choice of formats. This placement balances quick access and sufficient space. It feels more intuitive than the "View Full Content" modal.


**Finalizing File Downloads**

I'm deep into implementing the file generation logic now. I've built the `downloadStringAsFile` helper, which now handles the download functionality. For text formats (.txt, .md), the content is straightforward. For JSON, I'm using `JSON.stringify` for readable output. Images utilize Base64 data and an anchor tag, as planned. The `DownloadIcon` is added to my icon library, and the UI for the dropdown is underway inside the `SavedIdeaCard`. I've constructed the event handlers, including a basic filename sanitization for user-friendliness.


**Finalizing Image Downloads**

I'm wrapping up the final touches on file downloads. The download logic has been implemented.  I've refined the conditional logic for various file types and the image download now works correctly using `createObjectURL`. The download helper is complete, and the UI now shows the download menu on click. All formats seem working, time for a final review!


**Defining Download UI Logic**

I'm now fully immersed in the UI implementation for file downloads. I'm focusing on the interaction flow. The plan is to add a download button on the `SavedIdeaCard`. Clicking this initiates either an immediate image download or a dropdown menu to select text format, as I initially scoped. I'm writing the event handlers and considering how to make the dropdown position dynamic for different card sizes. I aim for minimal visual clutter while ensuring intuitive access.


**Integrating the UI**

I've added the `showDownloadMenu` state to control the visibility of the download menu. The action buttons section has been updated to include both the download button and the dropdown menu. This completes the core UI elements. Now I need to finalize the specific formatting logic, especially for the Outline to Markdown conversion, and confirm that image downloads work correctly. I need to make sure the implementation is accessible with proper aria labels, and visually refined with appropriate Tailwind positioning.


**Completing the Download Menu**

I've integrated the download menu fully into the `SavedIdeaCard`. I've linked the menu visibility to a new state variable.  The UI updates are finished, including positioning with Tailwind, and the menu appears as expected when the button is clicked. The next step is validating the generated files.

Tôi sẽ thêm tính năng lưu ý tưởng thành các định dạng tệp khác nhau (TXT, Markdown, JSON cho văn bản/dàn ý và PNG cho hình ảnh).

Dưới đây là các thay đổi chi tiết:

1.  **`components/Icons.tsx`**: Thêm icon `DownloadIcon`.
2.  **`components/SavedIdeaCard.tsx`**:
    *   Thêm nút tải xuống vào thanh công cụ.
    *   Thêm logic để chuyển đổi nội dung ý tưởng thành định dạng tương ứng (Formatter).
    *   Thêm menu dropdown nhỏ cho phép chọn định dạng (TXT, MD, JSON) khi tải xuống các ý tưởng dạng văn bản/dàn ý. Với hình ảnh, nó sẽ tải xuống ngay lập tức.

entries.description: Add download buttons with support for txt, md, json, and png

Các thay đổi bao gồm:
*   **`components/SavedIdeaCard.tsx`**: Cập nhật phần chân trang (footer) của modal xem chi tiết (`isViewingFull`). Thay vì chỉ một nút "Lưu .txt", tôi đã thêm một nhóm nút cho phép chọn định dạng tải xuống (TXT, MD, JSON) để thuận tiện hơn cho người dùng.

entries.description: Update the View Full modal to support downloading ideas in TXT, Markdown, and JSON formats.
